Printre aceştia se numără Dănuţ, un tânăr corporatist din Pipera poreclit de tot etajul „Psihopatul”. Potrivit mai multor colegi, acesta are obiceiul sadic de a-şi încheia mailurile şi mesajele […] The post Psihopatul din Pipera! romania.title: Noi reguli pentru ridicarea mașinilor parcate neregulamentar în București. Care este cel mai mic tarif de recuperare a autovehiculului
romania.summary: Primăria Municipiului Bucureşti a adoptat un nou regulament pentru ridicarea, transportul, depozitarea şi eliberarea vehiculelor staţionate neregulamentar pe domeniul public sau privat al Capitalei, împreună cu o structură tarifară unitară pentru toate sectoarele. Potrivit documentului, costurile variază în funcţie de masa vehiculului şi de durata în care acesta rămâne în depozit. Hotărârea a fost votată cu 42 de voturi pentru, 2 împotrivă.

romania.title: Cine este Viorel Pașca, acuzat că a exploatat sute de oameni vulnerabili în rețeaua sa de azile din Bihor
romania.summary: Asociația lui Viorel Pașca a fost vizată marți de zeci de percheziții ale Poliției Române, care îl acuză că a obținut sume imense de bani de pe urma a sute de oameni bolnavi, fără adăpost sau abandonați pe care i-a găzduit, fără autorizație, în căminele sale. Viorel Pașca, 55 de ani, este liderul Asociaţiei Dumbrava …

romania.title: 😂 Fanta anunță ca va cumpăra o nouă portocală, că aia din care a făcut suc între 1941 și 2026 s-a terminat
romania.summary: Compania Coca-Cola, producătorul celebrei băuturi Fanta, a anunțat oficial achiziționarea unei noi portocale pentru fabricarea sucului. Potrivit comunicatului oficial, portocala utilizată continuu ca materie primă începând din 1941 s-a epuizat complet în cursul anului 2026, după 85 de ani de producție neîntreruptă. Reprezentanții companiei au precizat că decizia vine după evaluări riguroase ale stocurilor existente […] The post Fanta anunță ca va cumpăra o nouă portocală, că aia din care a făcut suc între 1941 și 2026 s-a terminat appeared first on Times New Roman .

Pe lângă emoțiile și încurajările care au condimentat cursele din cadrul maratonului, pentru Fundația Motivation evenimentul a arătat așa: 14 utilizatori de scaun rulant... medical.title: Meaning of abstract art may be highly personal and connected to memory
medical.summary: Have you ever looked at an abstract painting and wondered what the artist was thinking? A splash of color on a canvas can stir something deep or nothing at all. According to research from Duke University, the difference may come down to something highly personal: your memory. The paper is published in the journal Memory & Cognition.

medical.title: Healthy but sedentary individuals show early decline in cellular energy production
medical.summary: Researchers at the University of Colorado Anschutz have found that healthy yet sedentary individuals show a significant, coordinated drop in muscle mitochondrial function that may precede the development of major diseases like cancer, diabetes, and Alzheimer's. The work is published in the journal Clinical Bioenergetics.

medical.title: Scalable mindfulness model can help treat chronic low back pain
medical.summary: Low back pain affects over 600 million people and is the single leading cause of disability worldwide. New research from Boston Medical Center (BMC), the largest essential hospital in New England, suggests that the Optimizing Pain Treatment In Medical settings Using Mindfulness (OPTIMUM) trial, in which an adapted group mindfulness program is delivered through primary care telehealth for patients with chronic low back pain, produced sustained reductions in pain and improvements in patients' daily lives. The findings, published in JAMA Internal Medicine, highlight a scalable, nonpharmacological approach to treating chronic pain.

medical.title: Photoswitch drug shows early signs of restoring light sensitivity in severely damaged retinas in first human trial
medical.summary: Adelaide University researchers have carried out the first in-human trial of a new type of treatment for a leading cause of blindness in working age adults, with promising results.

medical.title: Medical museums often display infant remains. How they were acquired was frequently harrowing
medical.summary: If you've been to a museum about the history of medicine or surgery, you've probably seen loads of preserved human remains that have been used as teaching aids or in scientific research.

medical.title: Cancer cell 'degraders' target two proteins at once to defeat treatment resistance
medical.summary: Researchers from two Technion faculties have jointly developed a new compound and demonstrated its effectiveness against aggressive tumor cells.

medical.title: How AI has moved from the sidelines to the center of health care
medical.summary: A new CSIRO report titled "AI Trends for Healthcare" reveals how AI is now embedded in real-world clinical settings for more connected, safer and more efficient health care.

medical.title: Mouse model supports long-held belief that viruses can trigger Parkinson's disease
medical.summary: Scientists usually use animal models when studying Parkinson's disease because these models mimic the disease well. They are limited, however, because they require either gene modifications or the injection of toxicants, which may not accurately represent how the disease occurs in humans. But now, researchers at Texas A&M University have developed a model that uses a nontoxic way to generate the symptoms of Parkinson's: infection with a virus called Theiler's murine encephalomyelitis virus (TMEV), a natural pathogen in mice.

medical.title: Same day administration of COVID-19 and influenza vaccines not associated with increase in adverse events
medical.summary: A target trial emulation found that same-day coadministration of the COVID-19 and influenza vaccines was not associated with a higher risk of serious or clinically meaningful adverse events compared with receiving only an influenza vaccine. Across multiple updated COVID-19 vaccine versions, overall safety outcomes were similar.

medical.title: Unlocking diabetes' secrets: Pediatric organ donors help map a path to a cure and prevention
medical.summary: The autoimmunity associated with type 1 diabetes often begins in the first years of life, and the diagnosis of type 2 diabetes is rapidly rising among children and teens.

medical.title: USC scientists just unlocked an endless supply of cancer-fighting immune cells
medical.summary: A new stem-cell-inspired technique allows scientists to grow vast numbers of immune-cell progenitors that can be engineered to hunt cancer and strengthen immune responses. In animal studies, the cells fought tumors, restored immune function, and showed promise as a durable, off-the-shelf therapy platform.

medical.title: Scientists discover an unexpected way to make pancreatic cancer cells self-destruct
medical.summary: Researchers tested experimental PCAI compounds against pancreatic cancer cells and found they had powerful anticancer effects. One leading compound blocked more than 90% of cancer cell migration, suggesting it could help prevent the spread of tumors. Rather than suppressing cancer signaling, the treatment hyperactivated key pathways until the cells essentially self-destructed.

science.title: New study of 2 million online posts shows persistent anti‑Jew and anti‑Muslim hate in Australia
science.summary: Australia has spent much of the past two years responding to anti-Jewish and anti-Muslim hate as separate problems. But our latest research suggests they have something important in common.

science.title: Isolation as a form of discipline: How should schools manage poor student behavior?
science.summary: Last week, a group of parents strongly criticized prestigious Victorian private school Geelong Grammar for using isolation as a form of discipline during a yearlong boarding school program. The disciplinary action was taken after a group of Year 9 students sneaked away to a nearby pub at night. As a result, some of the students were internally suspended for five days.

science.title: By 2050, many Sydney apartments built to today's standards could be too hot for weeks at a time
science.summary: Sydney is no stranger to extreme heat. In January 2020, Penrith in western Sydney reached 48.9°C, the highest temperature ever recorded in Greater Sydney.

science.title: Study investigates stress, coping among adult siblings of individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities
science.summary: Assistant professor of practice, psychology and human development, and VKC member Jessika Boles, Ph.D., and colleagues have published results of a research study detailing the complex lived experiences of adult siblings of individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD) in the journal Research in Developmental Disabilities.

science.title: How guaranteed income can support working families facing economic hardship
science.summary: An innovative partnership between George Mason University, Fairfax County and the United Way is shedding light on how guaranteed income can support working families facing economic hardship.

science.title: Stop the sting! Fire ant control tips
science.summary: Fire ants are a common nuisance across much of the United States, known for their painful stings and unsightly dirt mounds in lawns and outdoor spaces. However, for individuals who are allergic, these pests can cause potentially life-threatening reactions. To help keep communities safe this summer, University of Tennessee Extension is sharing tips for effective fire ant control.

science.title: One of the most distant 'leaky' galaxies ever found may reveal how the universe reionized
science.summary: Astronomers have identified one of the most distant candidate galaxies known to leak ionizing radiation—the same kind of radiation thought to have transformed the early universe during the epoch of reionization.

science.title: World Cup research reveals strategy to give teams a penalty-shootout edge
science.summary: One of football's most iconic moments—the penalty shootout—may be far more strategic than previously thought, with new research challenging the notion that the team kicking first holds a major advantage.

environment.title: Bornean ferret badger only lives in Borneo. Could it be a conservation symbol?
environment.summary: The Bornean ferret badger is a small carnivore with the slinky body of a ferret and a face mask like a badger. A new study confirms that it lives only in the mountains of Sabah, a Malaysian state on the island of Borneo. Ferret badgers are nocturnal carnivores, widespread across Southeast Asia, but the Bornean […]

environment.title: Mozambique completes first white rhino breeding population in decades
environment.summary: On June 6, nine female white rhinos arrived in Mozambique’s Zinave National Park following a two-day translocation. Their arrival marks the culmination of nearly 10 years of rhino reintroduction efforts in the park, aimed at rebuilding a viable breeding population of the mammals in Zinave after decades of local extinction. The white rhinos (Ceratotherium simum) […]

environment.title: ‘Flamingo Revolution’ aims to stop Kushner-backed resort on protected Albanian delta
environment.summary: VJOSA-NARTA, Albania — In late April, heavy machinery began moving into the Pishë Poro-Narta protected landscape on Albania’s Adriatic coast without permits or public notice. Bulldozers and excavators felled coastal pine trees, flattened sand dunes, and cut new roads through previously untouched habitat. Then, barbed wire fences went up along the shoreline. The incursion was […]

environment.title: The secret underground system keeping the Grand Canyon alive
environment.summary: Scientists are venturing into the Grand Canyon’s hidden cave networks to solve a mystery: how snowmelt travels underground to supply the park’s vital springs. Their discoveries could help protect the canyon’s water from drought, contamination, and other growing threats.

environment.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles’ lost crocodiles
environment.summary: Scientists have solved the mystery of the Seychelles’ vanished crocodiles using DNA from historic museum specimens. The reptiles were not a unique species after all, but an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles that likely drifted thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean.

environment.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
environment.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds. The research suggests these wrens are evolving independently, developing unique songs, appearances, and genetics that may eventually turn them into entirely new species.

environment.title: A New York cemetery was hiding 5.5 million bees underground
environment.summary: A casual walk through an Ithaca cemetery led to the discovery of a gigantic hidden bee population — roughly 5.5 million ground-nesting bees packed beneath the soil. Scientists believe it may be one of the largest bee aggregations ever documented and say the insects are crucial pollinators for apple orchards and other crops. The bees have likely lived there for more than 100 years, thriving in the cemetery’s undisturbed sandy soil.

environment.title: Earth’s orbital wobble triggered rapid climate chaos during the dinosaur age
environment.summary: New research suggests Earth’s climate can swing wildly on surprisingly short timescales — even during hot, ice-free greenhouse periods. By studying ancient sediments from the Late Cretaceous, scientists uncovered repeating climate shifts tied to tiny changes in Earth’s orbital wobble. These cycles may have repeatedly pushed the planet between humid and arid states every few thousand years.

Potrivit comunicatului oficial, portocala utilizată continuu ca materie primă începând din 1941 s-a epuizat complet în cursul anului 2026, după 85 de ani de producție neîntreruptă. mix_items.title: New study of 2 million online posts shows persistent anti‑Jew and anti‑Muslim hate in Australia
mix_items.summary: Australia has spent much of the past two years responding to anti-Jewish and anti-Muslim hate as separate problems. But our latest research suggests they have something important in common.

mix_items.title: Isolation as a form of discipline: How should schools manage poor student behavior?
mix_items.summary: Last week, a group of parents strongly criticized prestigious Victorian private school Geelong Grammar for using isolation as a form of discipline during a yearlong boarding school program. The disciplinary action was taken after a group of Year 9 students sneaked away to a nearby pub at night. As a result, some of the students were internally suspended for five days.

mix_items.title: By 2050, many Sydney apartments built to today's standards could be too hot for weeks at a time
mix_items.summary: Sydney is no stranger to extreme heat. In January 2020, Penrith in western Sydney reached 48.9°C, the highest temperature ever recorded in Greater Sydney.

mix_items.summary: Assistant professor of practice, psychology and human development, and VKC member Jessika Boles, Ph.D., and colleagues have published results of a research study detailing the complex lived experiences of adult siblings of individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD) in the journal Research in Developmental Disabilities.

mix_items.title: How guaranteed income can support working families facing economic hardship
mix_items.summary: An innovative partnership between George Mason University, Fairfax County and the United Way is shedding light on how guaranteed income can support working families facing economic hardship.

mix_items.title: Stop the sting! Fire ant control tips
mix_items.summary: Fire ants are a common nuisance across much of the United States, known for their painful stings and unsightly dirt mounds in lawns and outdoor spaces. However, for individuals who are allergic, these pests can cause potentially life-threatening reactions. To help keep communities safe this summer, University of Tennessee Extension is sharing tips for effective fire ant control.

mix_items.title: One of the most distant 'leaky' galaxies ever found may reveal how the universe reionized
mix_items.summary: Astronomers have identified one of the most distant candidate galaxies known to leak ionizing radiation—the same kind of radiation thought to have transformed the early universe during the epoch of reionization.

mix_items.title: World Cup research reveals strategy to give teams a penalty-shootout edge
mix_items.summary: One of football's most iconic moments—the penalty shootout—may be far more strategic than previously thought, with new research challenging the notion that the team kicking first holds a major advantage.

mix_items.title: Meaning of abstract art may be highly personal and connected to memory
mix_items.summary: Have you ever looked at an abstract painting and wondered what the artist was thinking? A splash of color on a canvas can stir something deep or nothing at all. According to research from Duke University, the difference may come down to something highly personal: your memory. The paper is published in the journal Memory & Cognition.

mix_items.title: Healthy but sedentary individuals show early decline in cellular energy production
mix_items.summary: Researchers at the University of Colorado Anschutz have found that healthy yet sedentary individuals show a significant, coordinated drop in muscle mitochondrial function that may precede the development of major diseases like cancer, diabetes, and Alzheimer's. The work is published in the journal Clinical Bioenergetics.

mix_items.title: Scalable mindfulness model can help treat chronic low back pain
mix_items.summary: Low back pain affects over 600 million people and is the single leading cause of disability worldwide. New research from Boston Medical Center (BMC), the largest essential hospital in New England, suggests that the Optimizing Pain Treatment In Medical settings Using Mindfulness (OPTIMUM) trial, in which an adapted group mindfulness program is delivered through primary care telehealth for patients with chronic low back pain, produced sustained reductions in pain and improvements in patients' daily lives. The findings, published in JAMA Internal Medicine, highlight a scalable, nonpharmacological approach to treating chronic pain.

mix_items.title: Photoswitch drug shows early signs of restoring light sensitivity in severely damaged retinas in first human trial
mix_items.summary: Adelaide University researchers have carried out the first in-human trial of a new type of treatment for a leading cause of blindness in working age adults, with promising results.

mix_items.title: Medical museums often display infant remains. How they were acquired was frequently harrowing
mix_items.summary: If you've been to a museum about the history of medicine or surgery, you've probably seen loads of preserved human remains that have been used as teaching aids or in scientific research.

mix_items.title: Cancer cell 'degraders' target two proteins at once to defeat treatment resistance
mix_items.summary: Researchers from two Technion faculties have jointly developed a new compound and demonstrated its effectiveness against aggressive tumor cells.

mix_items.title: How AI has moved from the sidelines to the center of health care
mix_items.summary: A new CSIRO report titled "AI Trends for Healthcare" reveals how AI is now embedded in real-world clinical settings for more connected, safer and more efficient health care.

mix_items.title: Mouse model supports long-held belief that viruses can trigger Parkinson's disease
mix_items.summary: Scientists usually use animal models when studying Parkinson's disease because these models mimic the disease well. They are limited, however, because they require either gene modifications or the injection of toxicants, which may not accurately represent how the disease occurs in humans. But now, researchers at Texas A&M University have developed a model that uses a nontoxic way to generate the symptoms of Parkinson's: infection with a virus called Theiler's murine encephalomyelitis virus (TMEV), a natural pathogen in mice.

mix_items.title: Same day administration of COVID-19 and influenza vaccines not associated with increase in adverse events
mix_items.summary: A target trial emulation found that same-day coadministration of the COVID-19 and influenza vaccines was not associated with a higher risk of serious or clinically meaningful adverse events compared with receiving only an influenza vaccine. Across multiple updated COVID-19 vaccine versions, overall safety outcomes were similar.

mix_items.title: Unlocking diabetes' secrets: Pediatric organ donors help map a path to a cure and prevention
mix_items.summary: The autoimmunity associated with type 1 diabetes often begins in the first years of life, and the diagnosis of type 2 diabetes is rapidly rising among children and teens.

mix_items.title: USC scientists just unlocked an endless supply of cancer-fighting immune cells
mix_items.summary: A new stem-cell-inspired technique allows scientists to grow vast numbers of immune-cell progenitors that can be engineered to hunt cancer and strengthen immune responses. In animal studies, the cells fought tumors, restored immune function, and showed promise as a durable, off-the-shelf therapy platform.

mix_items.title: Scientists discover an unexpected way to make pancreatic cancer cells self-destruct
mix_items.summary: Researchers tested experimental PCAI compounds against pancreatic cancer cells and found they had powerful anticancer effects. One leading compound blocked more than 90% of cancer cell migration, suggesting it could help prevent the spread of tumors. Rather than suppressing cancer signaling, the treatment hyperactivated key pathways until the cells essentially self-destructed.

mix_items.title: Bornean ferret badger only lives in Borneo. Could it be a conservation symbol?
mix_items.summary: The Bornean ferret badger is a small carnivore with the slinky body of a ferret and a face mask like a badger. A new study confirms that it lives only in the mountains of Sabah, a Malaysian state on the island of Borneo. Ferret badgers are nocturnal carnivores, widespread across Southeast Asia, but the Bornean […]

mix_items.title: Mozambique completes first white rhino breeding population in decades
mix_items.summary: On June 6, nine female white rhinos arrived in Mozambique’s Zinave National Park following a two-day translocation. Their arrival marks the culmination of nearly 10 years of rhino reintroduction efforts in the park, aimed at rebuilding a viable breeding population of the mammals in Zinave after decades of local extinction. The white rhinos (Ceratotherium simum) […]

mix_items.title: ‘Flamingo Revolution’ aims to stop Kushner-backed resort on protected Albanian delta
mix_items.summary: VJOSA-NARTA, Albania — In late April, heavy machinery began moving into the Pishë Poro-Narta protected landscape on Albania’s Adriatic coast without permits or public notice. Bulldozers and excavators felled coastal pine trees, flattened sand dunes, and cut new roads through previously untouched habitat. Then, barbed wire fences went up along the shoreline. The incursion was […]

mix_items.title: The secret underground system keeping the Grand Canyon alive
mix_items.summary: Scientists are venturing into the Grand Canyon’s hidden cave networks to solve a mystery: how snowmelt travels underground to supply the park’s vital springs. Their discoveries could help protect the canyon’s water from drought, contamination, and other growing threats.

mix_items.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles’ lost crocodiles
mix_items.summary: Scientists have solved the mystery of the Seychelles’ vanished crocodiles using DNA from historic museum specimens. The reptiles were not a unique species after all, but an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles that likely drifted thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean.

mix_items.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
mix_items.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds. The research suggests these wrens are evolving independently, developing unique songs, appearances, and genetics that may eventually turn them into entirely new species.

mix_items.title: A New York cemetery was hiding 5.5 million bees underground
mix_items.summary: A casual walk through an Ithaca cemetery led to the discovery of a gigantic hidden bee population — roughly 5.5 million ground-nesting bees packed beneath the soil. Scientists believe it may be one of the largest bee aggregations ever documented and say the insects are crucial pollinators for apple orchards and other crops. The bees have likely lived there for more than 100 years, thriving in the cemetery’s undisturbed sandy soil.

mix_items.title: Earth’s orbital wobble triggered rapid climate chaos during the dinosaur age
mix_items.summary: New research suggests Earth’s climate can swing wildly on surprisingly short timescales — even during hot, ice-free greenhouse periods. By studying ancient sediments from the Late Cretaceous, scientists uncovered repeating climate shifts tied to tiny changes in Earth’s orbital wobble. These cycles may have repeatedly pushed the planet between humid and arid states every few thousand years.

joke_ro.text: Azi n-am procrastinat deloc. Am amanat direct toata ziua.

satire_ro.title: Psihopatul din Pipera! Un corporatist îşi încheie mailurile cu „Salutări călduroase” şi „O zi însorită”
